steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: The passage claims males and females share the same sponge microhabitat—meaning both sexes must coexist in the same location. The data must show this cohabitation pattern across the sponges studied.

steps.title: Match the data pattern
steps.content: Choice C states that each of the three sponges was inhabited by at least one male and at least one female, directly proving that males and females cohabitate in multiple microhabitats. Choices A and B report only isolated counts—either males paired with undetermined sharks (A), or just a male number from Sponge 1 (B)—neither demonstrating the shared-habitat behavior. Choice D reports only females from Sponge 1, leaving the cohabitation pattern unaddressed. That matches choice C — C is correct.
